Chrysosplenium is a genus of flowering plants that are commonly known as golden saxifrage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysosplenium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chrysosplenium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although the effectiveness and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
